pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:10 creates patient with first_name='Maria'; confirmed in verifier.py:94 references patient 'Maria Santos'
setup_task.sh:10 creates patient with last_name='Santos'; confirmed in verifier.py:94 references 'Maria Santos'

setup_task.sh:22 defines PHARMACY_NAME='Rexall Pharma Plus' and creates it in pharmacy table at line 28. Rexall Pharma Plus is a real Canadian pharmacy chain (verified via web search: https://www.rexall.ca/), but this specific instance is synthetic.
setup_task.sh:28 inserts pharmacy record with telephone='905-555-0199'
setup_oscar.sh:295 sets browser startup homepage to this URL; task_utils.sh:140 uses identical URL; confirmed in Firefox desktop shortcut configuration at setup_oscar.sh:325
setup_oscar.sh:217 confirms oscardoc account configuration; setup_oscar.sh:373 prints 'Username: oscardoc'; verified in OSCAR EMR documentation that oscardoc is standard clinician user account

setup_oscar.sh:207-217 explicitly sets password hash for 'oscar'. Line 210 contains hash '45-179-551441115-117798-30877213-3052-6889-30-60' with comment 'Password oscar SHA-1 = ...'. Line 374 prints 'Password: oscar'. Note: Standard OSCAR default is 'mac2002' (per web search of open-osp documentation), but this deployment customizes it to 'oscar'.
setup_oscar.sh:217 confirms PIN configuration; setup_oscar.sh:375 prints 'PIN: 1117'; this matches standard OSCAR default PIN

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic OSCAR EMR deployment with auto-created test data. Patient 'Maria Santos' and pharmacy 'Rexall Pharma Plus' are created by setup scripts if they don't exist (setup_task.sh:10-32). The pharmacy is configured with phone '905-555-0199' (fictional test format). Credentials are oscardoc/oscar/PIN:1117, configured in setup_oscar.sh by setting SHA-1 password hash. OSCAR runs at http://localhost:8080/oscar/login.jsp. The task verifies successful linking in the demographicPharmacy database table by checking record existence, active status, and creation timing.
